spec.objective: Extend all four Job-creating controller reconcilers to inject OPENAI_API_KEY from project.Spec.CodexProviderSecretRef when the dispatched level resolves to Vendor:openai. Concretely: in task_controller.go, milestone_controller.go, phase_controller.go, and plan_controller.go, after the call to ResolveProvider, if Vendor is openai and project.Spec.CodexProviderSecretRef is non-empty, append an envFrom secretRef entry pointing at the named Secret to the Job pod spec alongside (not replacing) the existing credproxy env path. This mirrors the providerSecretRef pattern: the controller validates the Secret exists (Get; NotFound is a soft error with a requeue), then stamps the Secret name into the Job pod spec as envFrom. Add unit tests covering: (a) a task-level Job with vendor=openai gets the openai envFrom entry; (b) a phase-level planner Job with vendor=anthropic does NOT get the openai envFrom entry; (c) a missing CodexProviderSecretRef when vendor=openai logs a warning and requeues rather than crashing. Also update examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml to add codexProviderSecretRef: openai-secrets. make test must exit 0 and make verify-import-firewall must stay green (no codex package imports anywhere in the controller).
